methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $22M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 8 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $125M - $3.0B.

private.name: NODA AI
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Series A), post-money $125M

data.narrative:
Darkhive is a Series A-stage defense and security company founded by Steve Turner that has raised $22M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 10 comparable companies—2 public (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms in the same sector and stage, including NODA AI, Anduril Industries, Helsing, Shield AI, and Saronic.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $22M raised as a proxy for current value and applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A progression, yielding $44M. Peer-median analysis examines 8 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables with valuations ranging from $125M to $3.0B; the cohort median is $1.0B. Named peers include Dream Security ($3.0B, Series B), Harmattan AI ($1.4B, Series B), Code Metal ($1.2B, Series B), Castelion ($1.0B, Series B), and Defense Unicorns ($1.0B, Series B).

The two methods produce a wide range, reflecting typical uncertainty at early stage: low estimate $37M, median $522M, and high estimate $1.1B. The peer-median significantly exceeds stage-progression, suggesting either that Darkhive's peers have grown substantially post-Series A or that market multiples in defense tech have expanded. Key uncertainties include product maturity, revenue traction, customer pipeline, and whether Darkhive's technical differentiation aligns with the higher-valuation cohort or represents earlier-stage risk.
